item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one gives me anxiety. Easy +1. Shout-outs to RockTeethMothEyes for giving this article a deserved place in the Reviewer's Spotlight and bringing it to my attention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kind of a necropost, I know, but I just had to say how well-written I thought this is. I've read flash fiction of similar themes on other websites, and you have demonstrated that it is possible to do so in SCP format as well. I especially like how you captured the typical politician's 'not actually answering the question' response. I'm surprised that this is a first article for you. You certainly have talent.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
TL;DR: A unique approch that wouldn't work with most pages works very well with this one, and it pays off well, even though information about 4236 is very scarce.

It wasn't until I got to Addendum 02 that I understood a thing about what the device actually was doing…or more correctly, what it might be doing. I personally would swap Addendum 02 so that it came before Addendum 01, but that's just me.

A lot is left vague about this SCP, which is something that is surprisingly hard to find around this place (for better or worse). 4236-B is given only a brief description, and you end virtually ignoring the reason that B even (possibly) exists is because of all these papers appearing out of virtually nowhere.

It's a very odd approch to an SCP, and while I don't think a large amount of pages would be unable to get away with this sort of…"tactic", 4236 is able to squeak by and get away with it.
Good stuff. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is it weird that, for me, this is the scariest article I've read on the site? In the last addendum, the attitude of the family was, uh, far too real. Like people who don't understand that to our grandkids, "rain" will be something that happens somewhere else.

+1 for giving me the same sinking dread as 50-year climate models

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I seriously enjoyed the fact that you never really did tell us what -B does (though you did hint a bit). That was a bold artistic choice and I think it paid off in the grand scheme of the article because it ultimately doesn't matter. What matters is the cultural impact, how people react to this thing. You can draw any number of parallels between -B and tons of important world issues that are basically being straight up ignored or denied entirely in the world today, be it colonialism, nuclear expansion/MAD, climate change, radical political ideologies, and more.

Hearty +1. This was a fantastic article and I can't wait to see more!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You aren't reading too much into it, climate change anxiety is the direct inspiration for the article (though I wasn't necessary looking for it to serve as any kind of actual allegory as much as communicate that fear).

Someone else interpreted it as anxiety over nuclear proliferation and while I hadn't thought about that it works well with that analogy as well imo.

item.title: Nice job! +1
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this one. The idea itself is pretty interesting and you really did it justice! Its super well written and the characters presented feel organic, which made it super fun to read.

Definitely a +1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it's a not-very-subtle metaphor for climate change anxiety, yes? Maybe I'm reading too much into it

+1 regardless

This is a very interesting SCP and has a very good concept behind it. The Special Containment Procedures were very clear and understandable (because I've seen a lot of bad articles with bad Containment Procedures). Keep up the good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great stuff

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Thanks!

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded:
There is!

http://scp-wiki.net/black-highlighter-themes
How did you change the website format? Is there a template for that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel the clinical tone is rather lacking in much of the text but the concept and the execution on the final log bring this into +1 territory anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was quite enjoyable to read. The only real content critique I might have is with the length of the description, but it flowed well enough that I didn't really mind.

Overall, it captured what you set out to do quite well, excellent first work. +1

I wish I could've gotten a second look at this before posting, but in lieu of that, here are some minor formatting things:

+ Show nitpicks
- Hide nitpicks

The usual pattern for that header is:

OBJECT CLASS BAR
WIDE IMAGE
THREAT LEVEL BAR


Addendum 01: SCP-4236-00016 Military Engineering Report on the Construction and Activation of SCP-4236-B

Is a bit too bolded, only Addendum 01: should be bold, with the title being regular. This is similar to how Description: is bold but the actual description itself is not.

De-Classified Documents Reveal Military Science Experiment

I'm not a big fan of Wikidot headers, as I find them visually unattractive. I would recommend using a different format, like the newspaper in Hare 023: The Cheshire Cat.

item.title: A well deserved +1!
item.content:encoded:
This is an absolutely wonderful first SCP, and you've done an excellent job of tapping into the modern zeitgeist of an uncertain future, impending doom and consequential head-in-the-sandism of the general populace and burn out of activists.

The SCP itself works wonderfully as well, since it's legitimately unclear if reversing entropy would make life impossible or make us all immortal. It's believable that enough people in power would think it was worth the risk, and that most people would choose to accept that rather than risk wasting what could be their final days.

I look forward to reading more by you in the future!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good work, it's an interesting article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice job Legotrekker, very well done. I'm happy to see it on this site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yupes! I somehow missed this. Thanks so much. In the future I've got to make sure to document the individual web sources I get these things from so I don't run into that sort of thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just for reference, here's the image links! (Wikimedia Commons)

  • records2.jpg (PD)
  • The_Gadget.jpg (PD)
  • Leslie_Groves.jpg (PD)
  • obama.jpeg (PD)
  • gadget_0 (LANL requires the following text be used when crediting images to it:)
    • "Unless otherwise indicated, this information has been authored by an employee or employees of the Los Alamos National Security, LLC (LANS), operator of the Los Alamos National Laboratory under Contract No. DE-AC52-06NA25396 with the U.S. Department of Energy. The U.S. Government has rights to use, reproduce, and distribute this information. The public may copy and use this information without charge, provided that this Notice and any statement of authorship are reproduced on all copies. Neither the Government nor LANS makes any warranty, express or implied, or assumes any liability or responsibility for the use of this information."

I saw you posting this around looking for critique, but sadly I never got around to reading it in the draft stage.

I really like this skip. I like the fact that it’s a Keter which is not necessarily harmful and I (for once) like the usage of Threat Level to display this. The article is formatted exceedingly well, I like all the addenda and feel they add to it well.

The way people act in this article feels very realistic. You’ve presented members of the US Government in ways that I feel they would act in real life. Well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Giant thanks to anyone who made it to the end of this! As my first article, it's caused me a fair degree of anxiety, but it's also been an awesome learning process and I've enjoyed every anxious moment of it. I was hoping to invoke a very specific sense of discomfort bordering on terror that I've experienced a lot in the last few years with this one, and if even a few people got that same chill while reading I'll have done my job.

Photo attributions for this one are simple: All photographs used in this article were captured by the United States government and are considered to be works in the public domain.

Massive thanks and shoutout to all those who helped me get this written and published. IRC has been exceedingly friendly to me and there's been a whole lot of folks who contributed in small ways that I probably won't be able to recall. For greenlighting my idea and giving initial input, I give a huge thank-you to RockTeethMothEyes and N_Aepic_Fael. For their substantial critiques on my draft I need to thank aismallard, Ihp, Weryllium, and once again RockTeethMothEyes. In addition to the users above, I want to thank my friend Michael and my brother Mark for their interest, patience, and assistance with proofreading.

Whew long author post. Once again, hope you've all enjoyed this, and to see you again soon on whatever I put together next!
